channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A good rapport but mixed signals on Ukraine: Takeaways from Keir Starmer's trip to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prime Minister Keir Starmer wore a broad smile aboard the plane home from Washington. He landed back in Britain on Friday with the satisfaction of a tricky mission not quite accomplished, but off to a flying start.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Korean police summon Japanese woman for kissing BTS member without consent
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean police said Friday they are summoning a Japanese woman for questioning over allegedly kissing Jin, a member of the K-pop supergroup BTS, without consent during a free hug event last year.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Georgia deputy who fatally shot exonerated man during traffic stop won't be charged
item.description: District Attorney Keith Higgins announced, following the GBI's findings, they will not seek criminal charges against a Georgia sheriff’s deputy involved in a 2023 fatal shooting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the lives and deaths of Hollywood icon Gene Hackman and wife Betsy Arakawa
item.description: Gene Hackman, a Hollywood giant and two-time Oscar winner, was found dead along with his wife and dog in their New Mexico home Wednesday, though investigators believe they may have been dead for some time and the cause of death has yet to be revealed.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Cardinal McElroy calls for compassion in immigration policy as he prepares to lead in Washington
item.description: Cardinal Robert McElroy, who is getting ready to take over as archbishop of Washington, D.C. in March, called for compassion and dignity for migrants Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Law school project finds slavery citations still being used today
item.description: Students at Michigan State University's law school have found that slavery is still baked into American jurisprudence.
item.dc:creator: Corey Williams, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iowa gives final approval to a bill removing gender identity protections despite protests
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa lawmakers became the first in the nation to approve legislation removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code Thursday, despite massive protests by opponents who say it could expose transgender people to discrimination in numerous areas of life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Wendy Williams: 'I need a new attorney’ before cognitive test
item.description: Williams said her conservatorship feels "suffocating," comparing it to prison, and suspects her guardian may be misusing her money.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: No new bombshells in Justice Department’s release of Jeffrey Epstein files
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday released government documents related to wealthy financier Jeffrey Epstein, but the first wave of files posted on the Justice Department website have largely been circulating in the public domain for years and didn't include any new bombshells.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Mexico woman fatally shot, accused of leaving teen in chicken coop
item.description: Patricia Madrid and her husband, 59-year-old Jammy Jackson, were arrested after police said they left a 15-year-old in a chicken coop for long periods of time.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Honeycutt, NewsNation

item.title: Man on 'Most Wanted' list for over 4 decades caught in New Mexico
item.description: Investigators have been searching for Campbell since he vanished in 1983 while out on bond and facing charges of attempted first degree murder.
item.dc:creator: Bela Olague, NewsNation

item.title: Months into Hurricane Helene recovery, eight families to receive new homes
item.description: "I knew I didn't have flood insurance," said Patrick Pritchett. "So how am I going to do this? It was just unreal.
item.dc:creator: Madi Codispoti, NewsNation

item.title: Former sheriff ‘flabbergasted’ by alleged gun seizure incident
item.description: “It sounds like robbery,” Wilson’s attorney, Bobby Bailey, said. “To go to a person’s personal job and to take his personal items; to me, that’s robbery.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Man arrested in Nashville for impersonating law enforcement
item.description: According to police, 31-year-old Leart Bytyqi was identified as the man who tried to pull over a driver by flashing a badge out the car window.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Guerry, NewsNation

item.title: Man on 'Most Wanted' list for over 4 decades caught in New Mexico
item.description: After more than four decades, Stephen Craig Campbell, one of the United States' most wanted men, is now behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Bela Olague, ABC 4

item.title: Students escape school bus fire in Ohio
item.description: An emergency broke out Thursday morning on a school bus carrying children in Cleveland Heights, Ohio. An unexpected blaze — requiring a quick evacuation.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KRON4

item.title: EPA backtracks on Trump comment about cutting staff by 65% but says major spending cuts are coming
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Despite a comment by President Donald Trump, the Environmental Protection Agency does not plan to reduce its staff by 65%, the White House and the agency said Thursday, though major budget cuts are likely.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: NOAA begins firing hundreds of staffers
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) began the process of firing hundreds of probationary workers Thursday, the latest mass job cuts within federal agencies, three sources with knowledge of internal operations told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WGN 9

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein contact list: AG Pam Bondi releases highly anticipated DOJ documents
item.description: Many Jeffrey Epstein files released by the Justice Department Thursday had been made public in Ghislaine Maxwell's federal sex trafficking trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman arrested after explosives discovered at Tesla dealership
item.description: Forty-year-old Lucy Grace Nelson also known as Justin Thomas Nelson was charged with bringing explosives to a Colorado Tesla dealership after a series of incidents at the business.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police killings set record in 2024, but with smallest increase in years: Report
item.description: In 2024, police officers in the US killed 1,365 people, a slight increase from the 1,329 civilians killed in 2023, according to the Mapping Police Violence report.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: 3 councilmen in South Carolina's third-largest city charged in bribery and kickback scheme
item.description: Three members of the City Council in South Carolina's third-largest city accepted kickbacks and bribes to give out grant money and rezone land or lied about what they knew, federal prosecutors said Wednesday, Charges against North Charleston councilmen Jerome Heyward, Sandino Moses and Mike A.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote in an op-ed.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New details show Xanax found in Kansas City sports reporter's autopsy
item.description: The woman accused of theft in connection with the death of a Kansas City reporter in town for the Super Bowl was back in court on Tuesday, Feb. 25.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missouri woman accused in plot to sell Graceland says she'll plead guilty
item.description: A Missouri woman said Tuesday she is pleading guilty to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
